title: Merchandise Sale — Two Entries, Always
event: Reyes Co. sells merchandise for $5,000; the goods cost $3,200 (perpetual system). Prepare the entries.

entries.label: 1 — Record the sale at selling price

lines.label: retail price
lines.trap: Retail lives in entry 1 only — never let it leak into the cost entry.

lines.label: revenue at retail
lines.why: Revenue at RETAIL — what the customer pays.
lines.trap: Not 1,800, not 3,200 — revenue is the full selling price; profit emerges only after COGS.

lines.label: cost of goods delivered
lines.why: The goods' COST becomes expense the moment they sell.
lines.trap: The forgotten twin. Every perpetual sale has this second entry — exams love catching its absence.

lines.label: off the shelf at cost
lines.why: Goods leave the shelf at cost.
lines.trap: Credit Inventory at COST — crediting it at retail destroys the account.

entries.label: 1 — Record the sale at selling price

lines.label: retail price
lines.why: On account — the claim replaces the cash.
lines.trap: Only the debit changes between variants; revenue is identical.

lines.label: revenue at retail
lines.trap: Payment timing never changes the earning — chapter 2's lesson, still true.

lines.label: cost of goods delivered
lines.why: Cost flows out with the goods, regardless of when cash arrives.
lines.trap: On-account sales still need the cost entry immediately — COGS doesn't wait for collection.

lines.label: off the shelf at cost
lines.trap: Same trap as the cash case: cost, never retail.

memorize.body: Two prices live in every sale: RETAIL (entry 1, revenue) and COST (entry 2, COGS). Never mix them in one entry.
If your answer has one entry, it's half an answer.

questions.prompt: Sale $5,000, cost $3,200. Gross profit on this sale = ?

distractors.feedback: That's revenue before the cost of the goods. Gross profit nets out COGS: 5,000 − 3,200 = 1,800.
That's the COST of the goods. Gross profit is what's left of the selling price after it: 1,800.

build.scaffold: Entry 1 at retail, entry 2 at cost. If your answer has one entry, it's half an answer.
